title: Haplospondias haplophylla
intro: Haplospondias haplophylla, also known as the African bush mango, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive mango-like fruit and leaves with a unique, pinnate shape. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Haplospondias haplophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.

This family comprises over 130 genera and 1,500 species, with many of its members being found in tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific.
